Title: Structure and function of glycoglycerolipids in plants and bacteria

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Arabidopsis tgd1 mutant accumulates oligogalactolipids in vivo. 2. TGD1 functions in a lipid transport protein complex rather than as a galactolipid synthase. 3. Defect in TGD1 alters galactolipid homeostasis relevant to membrane lipid remodeling.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Defect in TGD1 causes accumulation of oligogalactolipids.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is a review paper; TGD1 is discussed as a transport-related factor and not a primary synthesis enzyme. The GGGT gene itself was not isolated.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for engineering plastid lipid transport and phosphate-responsive membrane remodeling, but likely pleiotropic.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: plant plastid MGD synthesis, type A branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers galactose from UDP-galactose to the sn-3 position of diacylglycerol to form monogalactosyldiacylglycerol via an inverting mechanism.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: major chloroplast MGD synthase; key control point for bulk MGD formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MGD1 is highly expressed in green tissues and throughout development. 2. MGD1 synthesizes the largest proportion of chloroplast MGD in Arabidopsis. 3. Type A MGDS is mainly localized to the inner envelope of plastids.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of the major chloroplast galactolipid precursor MGD.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene isolation; expression analysis; subcellular localization; mutant/biochemical characterization cited in review
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highly expressed in all developmental stages and in green tissues
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering chloroplast galactolipid content and photosynthetic membrane biogenesis.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced galactolipid synthesis impairs chloroplast function and growth.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Type A MGDS differs from type B MGDS enzymes in localization and tissue specificity.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major structural lipid for photosynthetic membranes and precursor of DGD

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: plant extraplastidial/phosphate-responsive MGD synthesis, type B branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers galactose from UDP-galactose to diacylglycerol to form MGD.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: glycosyltransferase family GT28, type B MGDS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MGD2 expression is restricted to specific organs including inflorescences and germinating pollen/pollen tubes. 2. MGD2 is associated with the plastid outer envelope. 3. MGD2 is highly upregulated during phosphate deprivation and likely provides MGD precursor for DGD synthesis outside canonical photosynthetic contexts.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports synthesis of extraplastidial galactolipids and phosphate-starvation-induced DGD production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: expression analysis; localization; physiological induction under phosphate deprivation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: transcripts localized to inflorescences; also expressed in germinating pollen and pollen tubes; induced by phosphate deprivation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: pollen tubes; extraplastidial membranes under phosphate deprivation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering phosphate-efficient membrane lipid remodeling and pollen tube membrane proliferation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altering expression may perturb tissue-specific membrane development.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Expression depends on auxin and cytokinin regulation under phosphate deprivation.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: plant extraplastidial/phosphate-responsive MGD synthesis, type B branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers galactose from UDP-galactose to diacylglycerol to form MGD.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: glycosyltransferase family GT28, type B MGDS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MGD3 is a type B MGDS with expression in roots and non-photosynthetic tissues. 2. MGD3 localizes to the plastid outer envelope. 3. MGD3 is strongly induced by phosphate deprivation and contributes MGD precursor for phosphate-starvation-associated DGD synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports phosphate-responsive galactolipid synthesis in roots and other non-photosynthetic tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: expression analysis; localization; physiological induction under phosphate deprivation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: root-specific among type B transcripts; germinating pollen and pollen tubes; induced by phosphate deprivation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: roots; extraplastidial membranes under phosphate deprivation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to improve phosphate deprivation tolerance through non-phosphorus membrane lipid substitution.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May affect root membrane composition and tissue-specific development.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Expression depends on auxin and cytokinin regulation under phosphate deprivation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers galactose from UDP-galactose onto MGD to form DGD via a retaining mechanism.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DGD1 was isolated through characterization of the Arabidopsis dgd1 mutant with reduced DGD. 2. DGD1 encodes the major DGD synthase responsible for cellular DGD production. 3. DGD1 expression increases under phosphate deficiency and contributes to DGD accumulation in plastidial and extraplastidial membranes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of DGD, a major bilayer-forming galactolipid important for photosynthesis and phosphate deprivation responses.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis; gene isolation; double-mutant analysis; biochemical characterization
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: plastid envelopes; thylakoids; extraplastidial membranes under phosphate deprivation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering phospholipid replacement and stress-adaptive membrane remodeling under phosphate limitation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced DGD lowers chlorophyll content, photosynthesis, chloroplast integrity, and growth.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: DGD1 contains an N-terminal extension required for plastid outer-envelope targeting.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports photosynthetic membrane structure and phospholipid substitution

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers galactose from UDP-galactose onto MGD to form DGD via a retaining mechanism.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DGD2 accounts for residual DGD synthase activity in the Arabidopsis dgd1 background. 2. Inactivation of DGD1 and DGD2 together abolishes DGD synthesis, showing DGD1 and DGD2 are sufficient for in vivo DGD production. 3. DGD2 is induced under phosphate deficiency and contributes to stress-responsive DGD accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides residual and phosphate-responsive DGD synthesis activity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis; double-mutant analysis; biochemical characterization
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: plastid envelopes; extraplastidial membranes during phosphate deprivation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be stacked with DGD1 and type B MGD synthases to enhance phosphate-saving lipid remodeling.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss together with DGD1 causes complete absence of DGD.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: DGD2 also contains an N-terminal plastid-targeting extension.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: cyanobacterial galactolipid biosynthesis via glucolipid intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: MGlcD formation prior to epimerization to MGD
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to the sn-3 position of diacylglycerol to form MGlcD via an inverting mechanism.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. In cyanobacteria, MGlcD rather than MGD is the first glycolipid intermediate. 2. MGlcDS genes were identified in Anabaena and Synechocystis by comparative genomics. 3. The enzyme prefers DAG species with C18 at sn-1 and C16 at sn-2 and is localized to cytoplasmic and thylakoid membranes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces the obligate glucolipid intermediate that feeds cyanobacterial MGD synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative genomic analysis; enzyme characterization; radiolabeling context
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: glucose supply can elevate MGlcD accumulation; heat/cold affect lipid unsaturation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for synthetic redesign of cyanobacterial or heterologous glycolipid pathways.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The epimerase converting MGlcD to MGD was not identified in this review.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers galactose to diacylglycerol to form MGD with a beta-glycosidic linkage.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The MGDS gene from Chloroflexus aurantiacus was isolated and characterized. 2. This enzyme resembles plant MGDS in specificity for beta-glycosidic bond formation. 3. It supports abundant MGD production in green non-sulfur bacteria.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes MGD accumulation in Chloroflexus membranes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene isolation; heterologous expression; enzyme activity characterization
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential donor enzyme for heterologous engineering of galactolipid synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review also reports heterologous use of a Chloroflexus glycosyltransferase in plants and cyanobacteria.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose onto MGD to form glucosylgalactosyldiacylglycerol with a beta(1→6) linkage.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A Chloroflexus aurantiacus glycosyltransferase forms beta-Glc(1→6)beta-GalD using MGD and UDP-Glc. 2. The enzyme is non-processive despite belonging to GT28. 3. Heterologous expression in cyanobacteria and plants enabled replacement of DGD with glucosylgalactosyldiacylglycerol in Arabidopsis dgd1.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables accumulation of glucosylgalactosyldiacylglycerol and partial functional replacement of DGD in Arabidopsis dgd1.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene isolation; heterologous expression in plants and cyanobacteria; lipid profiling; phenotypic complementation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Demonstrates feasibility of replacing native galactolipid head-group structure by heterologous glycolipids for membrane engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Only partial rescue of chlorophyll content and PSII quantum yield, indicating inferior substitution relative to native DGD.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper does not provide a specific gene symbol in the excerpt.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: alternative structural glycolipid in photosynthetic membranes

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SQD biosynthetic genes have been isolated from plants and cyanobacteria. 2. SQD synthesis is stimulated during phosphate deprivation. 3. In some bacteria SQD can substitute for phospholipids such as PG under low phosphate.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of SQD, supporting phosphate-saving membrane remodeling.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene isolation; targeted disruption; lipid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Targets for engineering phosphorus-efficient membranes in plants and microbes.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Disruption in Sinorhizobium revealed no clear symbiotic phenotype for one SQD gene.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific SQD gene names are not provided in the excerpt; included because the review explicitly discusses biosynthetic genes affecting nutrient-related membrane lipids.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: anionic membrane lipid supporting photosynthesis and phosphate adaptation

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: regulates expression of MGD2 and MGD3 under phosphate deprivation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Expression of Arabidopsis type B MGDS genes depends on auxin under phosphate deprivation. 2. This regulation links hormone signaling to phosphate-responsive galactolipid synthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes phosphate-responsive galactolipid synthesis via induction of type B MGDS genes.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Hormone-regulated lipid remodeling pathways could be exploited to improve phosphate efficiency.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Hormone manipulation may broadly affect growth and root architecture.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a gene/protein; included only because the review explicitly states regulatory dependence. No specific regulator gene is named.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: membrane lipid replacement during phosphate stress

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: regulates expression of MGD2 and MGD3 under phosphate deprivation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cytokinin regulates expression of Arabidopsis MGD2 and MGD3 during phosphate deprivation. 2. This connects hormonal signaling to phosphate-responsive membrane lipid remodeling.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Modulates phosphate-responsive galactolipid synthesis through type B MGDS expression.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: condition-dependent controller of phosphate-responsive galactolipid production
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Hormone-responsive lipid remodeling pathways may support breeding for low-phosphate adaptation.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Hormone pathway engineering may have pleiotropic developmental effects.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a gene/protein; no specific cytokinin-response regulator gene is named in the excerpt.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: membrane lipid replacement during phosphate stress
